issues.title: Calling Azure OpenAI O3-mini failed.
issues.body:
Please do a quick search on GitHub issues first, there might be already a duplicate issue for the one you are about to create.
If the bug is trivial, just go ahead and create the issue. Otherwise, please take a few moments and fill in the following sections:

**Bug description**
2025-03-10T14:40:26.199+08:00 ERROR 60063 --- [mqmanager] [nio-8087-exec-1] o.a.c.c.C.[.[.[/].[dispatcherServlet]    : Servlet.service() for servlet [dispatcherServlet] in context with path [] threw exception [Request processing failed: com.azure.core.exception.HttpResponseException: Status code 400, "{"error":{"code":"BadRequest","message":"Model {modelName} is enabled only for api versions 2024-12-01-preview and later"}}"] with root cause

**Environment**
		<dependency>
			<groupId>org.springframework.ai</groupId>
			<artifactId>spring-ai-azure-openai-spring-boot-starter</artifactId>
		</dependency>
		<dependency>
			<groupId>org.springframework.ai</groupId>
			<artifactId>spring-ai-openai-spring-boot-starter</artifactId>
		</dependency>		
	</dependencies>

	<build>
		<plugins>
			<plugin>
				<groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
				<artifactId>spring-boot-maven-plugin</artifactId>
			</plugin>
		</plugins>
	</build>


	<dependencyManagement>
		<dependencies>
			<dependency>
				<groupId>org.springframework.ai</groupId>
				<artifactId>spring-ai-bom</artifactId>
				<version>1.0.0-M6</version>
				<type>pom</type>
				<scope>import</scope>
			</dependency>
		</dependencies>
	</dependencyManagement>**Steps to reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the issue.

**Expected behavior**
just prompting

**Minimal Complete Reproducible example**
public class AIHelper {
    
    
    private final AzureOpenAiChatModel chatModel;
    
    @Autowired
    public AIHelper(AzureOpenAiChatModel chatModel) {
        this.chatModel = chatModel;
    }

    public String generate(String message) {
        return this.chatModel.call(message);
    }
    public Flux<ChatResponse> generateStream(String message) {
        Prompt prompt = new Prompt(new UserMessage(message));
        
        return this.chatModel.stream(prompt);
    }


}

comments.body:
Could you share your properties file? (without credentials)
The `{modelName}` placeholder appearing in the logs seems unnatural.
Was the log content manually edited before raising this issue?

> Could you share your properties file? (without credentials) The `{modelName}` placeholder appearing in the logs seems unnatural. Was the log content manually edited before raising this issue?

It's fine to  call gpt-4o , but failed for o3-mini.

```
spring.ai.azure.openai.api-key=xxx
spring.ai.azure.openai.endpoint=https://xxx.openai.azure.com
spring.ai.azure.openai.chat.options.deployment-name=o3-mini
spring.ai.azure.openai.chat.options.temperature=0.2
```
Just to confirm, was the `o3-mini` model deployed from your azure console?

> Just to confirm, was the `o3-mini` model deployed from your azure console?

yes, deployed on Azure AI Foundry. same as gpt-4o

This issue of API version management is common across various AI libraries, and most of them ultimately refer back to the official Microsoft documentation:

*   [Azure OpenAI API Version Deprecation](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/ai-services/openai/api-version-deprecation)
*   [Azure OpenAI API Reference](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/ai-services/openai/reference)

Spring AI specifically relies on the [Azure SDK for Java](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-java). You can see this dependency defined in the Spring AI `pom.xml` file [here](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-ai/blob/main/pom.xml#L193).

The supported API versions are defined as enum values within the Azure SDK for Java's `OpenAIServiceVersion.java` file: [Azure SDK OpenAI Service Versions](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-java/blob/main/sdk/openai/azure-ai-openai/src/main/java/com/azure/ai/openai/OpenAIServiceVersion.java).

To configure the Azure OpenAI client in your Spring application, you can use the `OpenAIClientBuilder`.  Here's an example configuration:

```java
@Bean
public OpenAIClientBuilder openAIClientBuilder() {
    return new OpenAIClientBuilder()
        .credential(new AzureKeyCredential(System.getenv("AZURE_OPENAI_API_KEY")))
        .endpoint(System.getenv("AZURE_OPENAI_ENDPOINT"))
        .serviceVersion(OpenAIServiceVersion.V2022_12_01) // Replace with your desired version
        .httpLogOptions(new HttpLogOptions()
            .setLogLevel(com.azure.core.http.policy.HttpLogDetailLevel.BODY_AND_HEADERS));
}
```

The latest version released 2 weeks ago: https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-java/releases/tag/azure-ai-openai_1.0.0-beta.14 and actually includes the latest preview api version `V2025_01_01_PREVIEW("2025-01-01-preview");`
https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-java/blob/azure-ai-openai_1.0.0-beta.14/sdk/openai/azure-ai-openai/src/main/java/com/azure/ai/openai/OpenAIServiceVersion.java#L86C5-L86C47

thus in short the azure sdk needs to be updated
